_description: Canonical entity registry for WARP authority page generation. Defines all entity families, their attributes, and knowledge graph relationships.

store-replenishment.label: Store Replenishment
store-replenishment.short_description: Scheduled multi-stop retail freight delivery using LTL and right-sized assets
warp_differentiators: Multi-stop route optimization across retail locations
Right-sized assets matched to pallet counts per stop
Appointment scheduling with live ETA visibility
Scan-level proof of delivery at each location

pool-distribution.label: Pool Distribution
pool-distribution.short_description: Consolidated freight pooling from distribution centers to regional delivery zones
warp_differentiators: Cross-dock pooling reduces per-unit freight cost
Flexible delivery windows at final destination
Carrier pool visibility across consolidation points
primary_use_cases: High-volume lanes with multi-destination final delivery

vendor-consolidation.label: Vendor Consolidation
vendor-consolidation.short_description: Multi-vendor pickup consolidation into single linehaul shipments
warp_differentiators: Multi-vendor pickup coordination on single route
Consolidation at origin reduces linehaul moves
Vendor compliance tracking with scan-level visibility
Predictable pickup windows across vendor locations
primary_use_cases: Retailer inbound freight from multiple vendors
Consolidated vendor pickups reducing receiving dock touches

zone-skipping.label: Zone Skipping
zone-skipping.short_description: Bypass carrier sort hubs by injecting freight closer to final delivery zones
warp_differentiators: Direct injection past carrier sort facilities
Reduced handling touches lower damage rates
Faster transit by eliminating intermediate terminals
Cost reduction through zone-based pricing optimization
primary_use_cases: Regional sort bypass for faster delivery

cross-docking.label: Cross-Docking
cross-docking.short_description: Direct transfer of freight between inbound and outbound vehicles without warehousing
warp_implementation: Strategic cross-dock locations in high-density freight corridors
Inbound/outbound coordination for same-day transfer
Scan-level tracking through cross-dock facility
No warehousing costs — freight moves directly to outbound

technical_depth.how_it_works: Freight arrives at a cross-dock facility and is sorted directly from inbound trailers to outbound vehicles without entering long-term storage. This eliminates warehousing costs and reduces transit time.
technical_depth.when_to_use: Best for high-velocity freight that doesn't require storage, value-added services, or picking. Ideal for retail replenishment, pool distribution, and consolidation programs.

middle-mile.label: Middle Mile
middle-mile.short_description: The linehaul segment between origin hub and destination hub before final delivery
warp_implementation: Dedicated middle-mile capacity on high-volume corridors
Trailer pool management for consistent linehaul availability
Real-time middle-mile tracking with milestone updates
Optimized routing to reduce empty miles on return legs

technical_depth.how_it_works: Middle mile is the longest-distance segment of a freight movement, connecting origin region to destination region. WARP operates dedicated middle-mile capacity on high-volume corridors with consistent trailer availability.
technical_depth.when_to_use: Critical for multi-leg freight movements where origin and destination are in different regions. Particularly important for pool distribution and zone-skipping programs.

scan-level-visibility.label: Scan-Level Visibility
scan-level-visibility.short_description: Item-level or pallet-level tracking with scan events at every handling point
warp_implementation: Barcode scanning at pickup, cross-dock, and delivery
Photo proof of delivery with timestamp and GPS coordinates
Real-time exception alerts for missed scans or delays
Shipper dashboard with live shipment status per SKU

technical_depth.how_it_works: Every handling event generates a scan record — pickup, cross-dock transfer, loading, delivery. Drivers capture barcode scans and photo POD at each stop. Data flows to a real-time dashboard accessible to shippers.
technical_depth.when_to_use: Essential for retail replenishment (planogram compliance), vendor-managed inventory, and any program where per-item or per-pallet accountability is required.

predictable-pricing.label: Predictable Pricing
predictable-pricing.short_description: Consistent rate structures based on lane commitment and volume patterns
warp_implementation: Lane-based pricing tied to committed volume, not spot markets
Rate lock programs with quarterly or annual terms
Transparent accessorial schedules with no hidden fees
Volume-based tiering that rewards consistency

technical_depth.how_it_works: WARP prices freight based on lane commitment and volume consistency rather than daily spot market fluctuations. Shippers who commit volume on specific lanes receive rate stability with published accessorial schedules.
technical_depth.when_to_use: Best for shippers with consistent lane patterns who need budget predictability. Particularly valuable for retail and CPG shippers with fixed distribution networks.

flexible-routing.label: Flexible Routing
flexible-routing.short_description: Dynamic route optimization based on real-time freight density and delivery requirements
warp_implementation: Dynamic route building based on daily freight density
Multi-stop optimization that adjusts to real-time volume
Same-day route changes without rebilling complexity
Pickup and delivery window flexibility within committed SLAs

technical_depth.how_it_works: WARP builds routes daily based on available freight density, delivery windows, and vehicle capacity. Routes are optimized in real-time as pickups confirm and volumes fluctuate throughout the day.
technical_depth.when_to_use: Best for multi-stop delivery programs where stop counts and volumes vary daily. Particularly effective for store replenishment and vendor consolidation where flexibility reduces empty miles.

right-sized-assets.label: Right-Sized Assets
right-sized-assets.short_description: Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency
warp_implementation: Vehicle selection from cargo van to 53-foot trailer based on cube and weight
No paying for unused trailer space on partial loads
Mixed fleet deployment across route stops
Automatic vehicle upgrade/downgrade based on volume changes

technical_depth.how_it_works: WARP's fleet spans cargo vans through 53-foot trailers. Each shipment is matched to the smallest vehicle that fits the load, so shippers don't pay for empty trailer space. Multi-stop routes may use different vehicle types across stops.
technical_depth.when_to_use: Critical for shippers with variable load sizes, especially retail replenishment where pallet counts differ per store. Also valuable for shippers currently overpaying for full trailers on partial loads.

cargo-van.label: Cargo Van
cargo-van.short_description: Enclosed cargo van for small-freight and last-mile delivery
best_fit_freight: Expedited local freight within metro areas
High-value, low-weight shipments requiring direct handling
not_ideal_for: Standard pallet loads requiring dock-height access

box-truck.label: Box Truck
box-truck.short_description: Medium-duty box truck (16-26 ft) for mid-size loads and multi-stop routes
best_fit_freight: Multi-stop retail delivery routes (4-12 pallets per stop)
Store replenishment at locations without dock access
Freight requiring liftgate for ground-level delivery
not_ideal_for: Bulk commodities requiring maximum cubic capacity

53-foot-trailer.label: 53-Foot Trailer
53-foot-trailer.short_description: Standard full-size dry van trailer for high-volume linehaul and FTL freight
not_ideal_for: Small loads under 6 pallets (overpaying for space)
Urban areas with vehicle length restrictions

_relationship_index._description: Pre-computed bidirectional relationship index for O(1) graph traversal. Rebuild with authority-graph.js rebuildIndex().
